attemptedTheorem.candidateStatement: For the 32 mod 50 top repair-class lane, the symbolic bad-lane congruence plus the compact 1..40500 finite base controls all relevant square-witness obstructions needed for the top repair-class coverage theorem.
attemptedTheorem.reason: The available schema is universal for divisibility by a named witness modulus Q, but the artifact surface does not quantify or cover all relevant future representatives n and square witness moduli Q.

symbolicBadLaneSchema.symbolicStatement: For any representative n and square witness modulus Q, continuation c = 32 + 50*m has Q | c*n + 1 exactly when (50*n)*m ≡ -(32*n + 1) (mod Q). When this congruence is solvable, it defines the bad m-class modulo Q/gcd(50*n,Q).

theoremBoundary.reason: This packet connects the symbolic bad-lane schema to the finite exchange surface, but it does not prove that all post-11300 candidates are controlled by the strict handoff family.

laneIndexSurface.interpretation: The finite handoff proves selected bad-m-class avoidance for checked witnesses, but does not prove that every future bad m-class is avoided by a top-tie continuation or controlled by a finite handoff family.

missingHypotheses.requiredStatement: Classify or finitely cover every relevant pair (n, Q), where n is a future/family representative and Q is a square witness modulus that can obstruct a continuation in c = 32 + 50*m.
missingHypotheses.currentEvidence: Only 74 finite witness instances are checked, with observed periods 9, 49, 169, 289, 361, 529, 1369, 1681, 4489, 66049.
missingHypotheses.whyNeeded: Without this domain cover, the universal divisibility schema cannot become a universal squarefree repair theorem.

missingHypotheses.requiredStatement: For every covered (n, Q), prove that the bad m-class modulo Q/gcd(50n,Q) is avoided by the selected top-tie lane index, or is handled by a finite handoff rule such as 782 vs 1232.
missingHypotheses.currentEvidence: Top lane indices are 432->m8, 782->m15, 832->m16; the strict 782 vs 1232 handoff covers 2 finite rows.
missingHypotheses.whyNeeded: The finite +2 separation does not by itself show how future bad lane classes are routed.

missingHypotheses.requiredStatement: Bridge the restored finite-menu predicted-family repair counts to the exact breakpoint rows certified by the compact endpoint certificate.
missingHypotheses.currentEvidence: The compact audit certifies 810 lane-32 breakpoint rows through 40500, but all same-lane top and contrast continuations are certified by the same 7 mod 25 endpoint class.
missingHypotheses.whyNeeded: The exact verifier confirms extremality of the residue class, not the top-repair leaderboard separation.

missingHypotheses.requiredStatement: Prove why checking the compact base through 40500 plus the symbolic lane theorem suffices beyond 40500, or state the next finite regression interval with a theorem-facing reason.
missingHypotheses.currentEvidence: The endpoint audit found no failed boundary row and explicitly does not justify a blind 40501+ rollout.
missingHypotheses.whyNeeded: A raw interval extension without this sufficiency theorem would repeat bounded verification without compressing the proof.

exactUncoveredWitnessLaneFamily.uncoveredScope: All future or unenumerated representatives n and square witness moduli Q outside the 74 checked finite witness instances, including any Q not in the observed finite period set and any observed-period instance not proved to belong to a covered handoff family.

blocker.deterministicBoundary: The route is blocked exactly at the missing square-witness domain cover and bad-lane handoff cover for c = 32 + 50*m. The existing finite instances remain valid evidence, but they cannot be promoted to all relevant square-witness coverage.

proofBoundary: This packet blocks the mod-50 lane coverage theorem at the hypotheses layer. It preserves the universal divisibility schema and the compact 1..40500 finite base, but refuses to claim universal squarefree repair, post-40500 breakpoint sufficiency, a 40501+ rollout justification, p4217 complement closure, or an all-N decision.
nextTheoremMove: Derive a square-witness domain cover for the 32 mod 50 lane: parameterize all relevant (n, Q, bad m-class) families beyond the 74 finite instances and prove the top-tie/handoff lane indices cover them, or emit a counterfamily/hypotheses packet before any 40501+ rollout.
